Package simulation

Interface NodeDeploymentStrategy

Functional Interface:
This is a functional interface and can therefore be used as the assignment target for a lambda expression or method reference.
@FunctionalInterface public interface NodeDeploymentStrategy
Strategy for deploying sensor nodes (or other targets) into the simulation environment at the start of each pass.

Built-in implementations

  • random() — fully random placement; any position in the field is equally likely. Can produce clusters and sparse regions by chance, making results sensitive to which seed is used.
  • stratified() — stratified random sampling (jittered grid); divides the field into a grid of equal cells and places exactly one node at a random position within each cell. Guarantees spatial uniformity regardless of seed while retaining per-run randomness. Recommended for scientifically credible experiments.

Custom strategies

   // Cluster nodes in the bottom-left quadrant
   NodeDeploymentStrategy clustered = (env, config, rng) -> {
       int halfW = config.getFieldWidth()  / 2;
       int halfH = config.getFieldHeight() / 2;
       int placed = 0;
       while (placed < config.getNodeCount()) {
           int x = rng.nextInt(halfW);
           int y = rng.nextInt(halfH);
           NodeAgent a = new NodeAgent(config.getNodeIdStart() + placed,
                                       x, y,
                                       rng.nextInt(NodeAgent.DIRECTION_COUNT),
                                       config.getNodeMinSpeed());
           if (env.deployAgent(a)) placed++;
       }
   };

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    deploy(Environment env, SimulationConfig config, Random rng)
    Deploys exactly SimulationConfig.getNodeCount() agents into env using rng for all random decisions.
    static NodeDeploymentStrategy
    random()
    Fully random placement: each node is placed at a uniformly random position anywhere in the field.
    static NodeDeploymentStrategy
    stratified()
    Stratified random sampling (jittered grid): divides the field into a near-square grid of cells and places exactly one node at a random position within each cell.

  • Method Details

    • deploy

      void deploy(Environment env, SimulationConfig config, Random rng)
      Deploys exactly SimulationConfig.getNodeCount() agents into env using rng for all random decisions.
      Parameters:
      env - environment to deploy into
      config - simulation parameters (node count, field size, speeds, etc.)
      rng - seeded random generator shared with the rest of the pass; all random decisions must go through this instance for reproducibility

    • random

      static NodeDeploymentStrategy random()
      Fully random placement: each node is placed at a uniformly random position anywhere in the field.

      Simple but can produce clusters and sparse regions by chance, making results sensitive to the choice of seed. Use stratified() for experiments that require seed-independent spatial uniformity.

    • stratified

      static NodeDeploymentStrategy stratified()
      Stratified random sampling (jittered grid): divides the field into a near-square grid of cells and places exactly one node at a random position within each cell.

      Why this matters scientifically

      Pure random placement is spatially uniform in expectation but produces clusters and voids in any individual run. Strategy A might happen to cover a dense cluster that Strategy B misses purely by chance, skewing the comparison. Stratified sampling guarantees every region of the field receives exactly one node, making results independent of which seed is used and removing the need to cherry-pick "good" seeds.

      Example — 225 nodes, 1500×1500 field

      Grid: 15 columns × 15 rows, each cell 100×100 units. Every 100×100 region gets exactly one randomly-positioned node. The seed still controls where within each cell the node lands, plus initial direction and speed, so results vary across seeds while remaining spatially fair.
